title: Punishments
content: The punishments to which offenders are liable under the provisions of this Code are: Firstly, Qisas; Secondly, Diyat; Thirdly, Arsh; Fourthly, Daman; Fifthly, Ta'zir; Sixthly, Death; Seventhly, Imprisonment for life; Eighthly, Imprisonment which is of two descriptions, namely: (i) Rigorous, i.e., with hard labour, (ii) Simple; Ninthly, Forfeiture of property; Tenthly, Fine.

title: Commutation of sentence of death
content: The Federal or Provincial Government may, without the offender's consent, commute the death sentence for any other punishment.

title: Commutation of sentence of imprisonment for life
content: The Government may commute a sentence of imprisonment for life for imprisonment of either description for a term not exceeding twenty years.

title: Definition of appropriate Government
content: The 'appropriate Government' means the Federal Government in cases where the sentence is one of death or for any other offence against laws relating to a matter to which the executive power of the Federal Government extends.

title: Sentence of Europeans and Americans to penal servitude
content: Repealed by the Criminal Law Amendment Act, 1921.

title: Fractions of terms of punishment
content: In calculating fractions of terms of punishment, imprisonment for life shall be reckoned as equivalent to imprisonment for twenty-five years.

title: Omitted/Repealed Sections
content: Sections 58 through 62 were repealed/omitted by the Federal Laws (Revision and Declaration) Ordinance, 1981.

title: Amount of fine
content: Where no sum is expressed to which a fine may extend, the amount of fine to which the offender is liable is unlimited, but shall not be excessive.

title: Solitary confinement
content: The Court may order an offender to be kept in solitary confinement for any portion of their rigorous imprisonment, not exceeding three months in total.

title: Limit of solitary confinement
content: In executing a sentence of solitary confinement, such confinement shall in no case exceed fourteen days at a time.

title: Enhanced punishment for certain offences under Chapter XII or Chapter XVII after previous conviction
content: Whoever, having been convicted of an offence punishable under Chapter XII or Chapter XVII with imprisonment of either description for a term of three years or upwards, shall be guilty of any offence punishable under either of those chapters with imprisonment of either description for a term of three years or upwards, shall be subject for every such subsequent offence to imprisonment for life, or to imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years.
